gaps: Live API testing skipped — user has no API key; verification limited to documented surface and mock responses
predict-return and reliability features require ≥3 years of synced gigography to produce meaningful output
drift watcher returns empty until at least one prior sync snapshot exists in the store
patterns: Wrapped resultsPage envelope on every response
Hard 50-row per_page cap requires client-side page stitching

novel_features.name: Tour-routing scan
novel_features.description: Score an artist for routing feasibility against a target date and anchor city. Returns nearest show, daysFromTarget, distanceKm, score, and a strong-fit / considered / cold verdict.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-joins synced artist calendar + great-circle distance + signed day-delta + 60/40 scoring formula in one SQL pass. The Songkick API returns raw events; the scoring requires local geo and time math.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a booking team needs to decide if a target artist is worth a cold pitch or a routing offer. Strong-fit verdict = artist is already in the region.

novel_features.name: Routing-gap detector
novel_features.description: Find date windows where an artist plays in the region but leaves a slot unbooked near the anchor city — the cheapest pitch opportunity.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local interval math across the artist's calendar; impossible without a time-series of their tour dates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you want to know not just whether the artist is nearby, but whether there is an open day in their schedule that could absorb a Jakarta date.

novel_features.name: Predictive return window
novel_features.description: Mine an artist's multi-year gigography for median inter-visit interval and seasonality patterns to forecast their next probable window in a given city.
novel_features.rationale: Requires multi-year gigography in local store; no single API call exposes inter-visit cadence or seasonality.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when planning a multi-quarter calendar; tells you when an artist is overdue for a regional visit.

novel_features.name: Festival lineup overlap
novel_features.description: Compare performances across multiple festival event IDs to surface shared artists, billing-order deltas, and headliner-vs-mid drift.
novel_features.rationale: Joins performances across N events using denormalized local performances table; pure set algebra impossible from raw API responses.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during festival programming to check differentiation against competing festivals in the same season.

novel_features.name: Cancellation reliability index
novel_features.description: Compute an artist's cancellation and postponement rate over a multi-year window, with region clustering and recovery-time analysis.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating event status (ok / cancelled / postponed) across an artist's full event history — not surfaced by any single API endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before signing a deposit-heavy headliner. High cancellation rate = higher booking risk.

novel_features.name: Market saturation heatmap
novel_features.description: Per-week show-count density for a metro area, capacity-weighted and venue-tier-segmented across a date range.
novel_features.rationale: Joins metro calendar with local venue capacity + tier tables — single SQL aggregation that the raw API can not return.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to answer 'is this date already crowded?' before committing budget to a competing event.

novel_features.name: Venue-tier classifier
novel_features.description: Cluster a metro area's venues into S / A / B / C tiers using capacity quantiles, headliner-billing frequency, and average popularity.
novel_features.rationale: Persistent tier labels computed locally from venue + performances + events tables; foundational input to other commands.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this once per metro to label your venue inventory; downstream commands (route, saturation, fit) consume the tier labels.

novel_features.name: Co-touring artist graph
novel_features.description: Build an artist graph from shared performances; surface support-act candidates and stylistic neighbors out to N hops.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local graph computation across the performances table; depth-2 traversal would mean dozens of API round-trips otherwise.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to find support acts that have already shared bills with the headliner, or to discover stylistic neighbors for an unsigned slot.

novel_features.name: Routing batch from shortlist
novel_features.description: Run the routing-feasibility score across an entire shortlist of artists in one SQL query; sorts by verdict and emits NDJSON for downstream agents.
novel_features.rationale: API-only approach means N round-trips with no scoring; local store + single SQL aggregation finishes in milliseconds for hundreds of artists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you have a 50-200 artist shortlist and need to rank them by routing economics for a specific event date.

novel_features.name: Drift watcher
novel_features.description: Diff current snapshot against prior — surface newly added shows near the anchor, capacity changes, billing-order promotions, and status flips since the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Requires snapshot history table populated on every sync; impossible without persistent state between API calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during the daily-refresh cycle to surface what changed that actually affects the booking team's workflow today.

novel_features.name: Headliner-promotion tracker
novel_features.description: Walk an artist's gigography ordered by date and detect the inflection point where their billing_index crossed into the headliner slot.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local timeline analysis on the performances table; not derivable from any single API endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when scouting emerging artists — surfaces 'about to break' candidates whose billing position is climbing.

novel_features.name: Festival-fit scorer
novel_features.description: Score an artist shortlist against a festival brief (tier mix, budget caps, genre proxies) by joining venue-tier, popularity, routing feasibility, and co-tour adjacency.
novel_features.rationale: Composite of every other transcendence feature; requires all local tables joined in one pass.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this as the final lineup-planner pass — emits ranked artist-to-event fit scores agents can hand to the booking team.

narrative.headline: The Songkick CLI for music-agency tour routing — every documented endpoint, a local SQLite store, and routing intelligence that no wrapper ships.
narrative.value_prop: Brings every documented Songkick v3.0 endpoint into a single Go binary with offline FTS search, persistent SQLite store, and great-circle distance math. Pairs the documented surface with twelve novel commands (`route`, `gap`, `predict-return`, `reliability`, `saturation`, `venue-tier`, `co-tour`, `route-batch`, `drift`, `promotion`, `lineup-overlap`, `fit`) built for festival programmers who need to answer routing and lineup questions in seconds, not API round-trips.
narrative.auth_narrative: Songkick's public API key program is effectively closed to new applicants — bring your own legacy or partner key via the `SONGKICK_API_KEY` environment variable. The CLI never sends the key anywhere except `api.songkick.com/api/3.0`.

quickstart.comment: set SONGKICK_API_KEY env var first; this verifies auth + reachability
resolve an artist name to a Songkick ID
fetch upcoming shows for the artist (populates local store)
score the artist for a Jakarta date

troubleshoots.fix: Export SONGKICK_API_KEY with a valid key. Songkick closed new-applicant access ~2017; legacy/partner keys still work.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty result from `route` for an artist you know is touring
troubleshoots.fix: Run `songkick-pp-cli sync --artist <id>` first — `route` reads from the local store and needs that artist's calendar synced.
The Songkick API caps `per_page` at 50. The CLI auto-stitches pages; pass `--limit 0` to fetch all pages, or set a higher `--limit`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Stale data after Songkick made site changes
troubleshoots.fix: Songkick was acquired by Suno in Nov 2025; surface changes may happen. Run `songkick-pp-cli doctor` to confirm endpoint reachability.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when you need to feed a booking team or tour-routing workflow with structured Songkick data — routing-feasibility checks for a target date, shortlist scoring, gigography-derived risk signals, or festival lineup analysis. It assumes you already hold a legacy Songkick API key. Pair with the Bandsintown CLI for cross-source confirmation.

recipes.title: Find routing-feasible artists for a Jakarta event
recipes.explanation: Scores an entire shortlist against a target date and emits NDJSON ready for downstream agents to filter.

recipes.title: Detect open windows in a target artist's tour
recipes.explanation: Returns date windows where the artist plays in the region but leaves a slot unbooked near Jakarta — the cheapest pitch opportunity.

recipes.title: Tier-label every Jakarta venue
recipes.explanation: Classifies Jakarta venues into S/A/B/C tiers and persists the labels for downstream `saturation` and `fit` commands.

recipes.title: Check festival lineup overlap with a competitor
recipes.explanation: Surfaces shared artists between two festivals' performance lists for differentiation analysis.

recipes.title: Detect what changed near the anchor since last sync
recipes.explanation: Diffs the last week of sync snapshots and emits booking-team-relevant changes only.

trigger_phrases: is this artist touring in southeast asia
